content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "financial-engine",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 68,
    "data_richness": 55,
    "interaction_quality": 52,
    "ai_integration": 35,
    "mobile_first": 40,
    "performance": 58,
    "typescript_quality": 64,
    "error_handling": 56,
    "realtime": 42,
    "accessibility": 30,
    "security": 55,
    "differentiation": 48,
    "enterprise_readiness": 45
  },
  "overall_score": 51,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "SCurve chart SVG missing ARIA roles and keyboard-navigable data points",
      "file": "src/components/budget/SCurve.tsx",
      "description": "The SVG chart uses role='img' but data points lack individual aria-labels, and the keyboard navigation via onKeyDown on the parent div does not announce hovered values to screen readers. No aria-live region announces the current data point when navigating with arrow keys.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/budget/SCurve.tsx, in the SCurve component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The parent div has onKeyDown that updates `hovered` state but there is no aria-live region to announce the selected data point value to screen readers. Data point circles in the SVG have no aria-label attributes. AFTER: (1) Add a visually hidden aria-live='polite' div below the SVG that renders the currently hovered month and value text, e.g. `{months[hovered]}: Actual $${actualData[hovered]}M, Planned $${plannedData[hovered]}M`. (2) Add role='listitem' and aria-label={`${months[i]}: $${actualData[i]}M actual`} to each actual data point circle element in the SVG. (3) Add tabIndex={0} and aria-roledescription='interactive data chart' to the SVG element itself. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Pay Applications page has no loading skeleton or empty state per acceptance criteria",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/payApplications.ts",
      "description": "The getPayApplications function returns an empty array on no data and throws on error, but the page component that consumes this does not differentiate between loading, empty (no SOV), and empty (SOV exists but no pay apps). PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA item 12 requires a prerequisite check for schedule of values before allowing pay app creation.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/payApplications.ts, in the getPayApplicationsWithSOVCheck function (or create it if it only exists as getPayApplications), make these specific changes: BEFORE: The function getPayApplications returns PayApplication[] but does not indicate whether schedule of values (budget_items) exist for the project. The consuming UI cannot distinguish 'no SOV configured' from 'SOV exists but no pay apps yet'. AFTER: Export a new function `getPayApplicationsWithContext` that performs two queries: (1) fetch pay_applications for the project, (2) fetch count of budget_items for the project. Return type should be `{ payApps: PayApplication[], hasScheduleOfValues: boolean, isLoading: false }`. If budget_items count is 0, set hasScheduleOfValues to false so the UI can show 'You need a schedule of values before creating a pay app' with a link to SOV setup, matching acceptance criteria item 12. Keep the existing getPayApplications function unchanged for backward compatibility. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Treemap chart has no responsive layout for mobile viewports",
      "file": "src/components/budget/Treemap.tsx",
      "description": "The Treemap component checks window.innerWidth for isMobile but the drill-down child view and the main treemap layout do not adapt to single-column on mobile. Touch targets for small division cells are below the 44x44px minimum required by Apple HIG and the design standards.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/budget/Treemap.tsx, in the Treemap component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component sets isMobile state based on window.innerWidth < 768 but does not use it to change the layout. Division cells in the treemap can be very small on mobile screens making them impossible to tap. There is no minimum height or width enforced on cells. AFTER: (1) When isMobile is true, render the divisions as a vertical list of cards instead of a proportional treemap grid. Each card should show the division name, budget amount formatted with fmt(), a progress bar showing spent/budget ratio, and be full width with minHeight 56px to ensure adequate touch targets. (2) Each card should be tappable (onClick to drill down, same as current behavior). (3) Add aria-label to each division cell/card: `${div.name}: ${fmt(div.budget)} budget, ${fmt(div.spent)} spent`. (4) When not mobile, ensure each treemap cell has a minimum width and height of 44px. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "computeCurrentPaymentDue uses incorrect retainage formula per AIA G702 standard",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/payApplications.ts",
      "description": "The computeCurrentPaymentDue function calculates retainage on cumulative work (previousWork + workThisPeriod) rather than on totalCompletedAndStored. Per AIA G702, retainage applies to the full total of completed work plus stored materials (line 5), not just work completed. Also, previousCertificates should represent the total of all prior net payments, not just prior retainage.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/payApplications.ts, in the computeCurrentPaymentDue function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: retainageOnWork is calculated as `(previousWork + workThisPeriod) * retainageRate` and retainageOnStored as `storedMaterials * storedMaterialRetainageRate`. The currentPaymentDue formula is `totalCompletedAndStored - retainageOnWork - retainageOnStored - previousCertificates`. This does not match the AIA G702 form where Line 5 = Total Completed and Stored to Date, Line 5a = Retainage on completed work (% of Line 5 minus stored materials), Line 5b = Retainage on stored materials (% of stored materials), Line 6 = Total earned less retainage (Line 5 - Line 5a - Line 5b), Line 7 = Less previous certificates for payment, Line 8 = Current payment due (Line 6 - Line 7). AFTER: Rename the calculation variables to match AIA G702 line numbers for clarity. Calculate: `const line5 = totalCompletedAndStored` (which is already correct). `const line5a = Math.round((line5 - storedMaterials) * retainageRate * 100) / 100` (retainage on completed work only). `const line5b = Math.round(storedMaterials * storedMaterialRetainageRate * 100) / 100`. `const line6 = Math.round((line5 - line5a - line5b) * 100) / 100`. `const currentPaymentDue = Math.round((line6 - previousCertificates) * 100) / 100`. Update the return type to include `line5`, `line5a`, `line5b`, `line6` as additional fields so the UI can render the full G702 breakdown. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "financial-engine-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "WaterfallChart lacks hover tooltips and click interaction for change order drill-down",
      "file": "src/components/budget/WaterfallChart.tsx",
      "description": "The WaterfallChart renders static bars with no hover state, no tooltips showing exact values, and no click handler to navigate to change orders. Per design standards, every chart element should be hoverable with exact values shown, and the Approved COs bar should link to the change orders list filtered by approved status.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/budget/WaterfallChart.tsx, in the WaterfallChart component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: Bars are static divs with no onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave handlers, no tooltip display, and no onClick navigation. The bar elements have no cursor:pointer styling or hover visual feedback. AFTER: (1) Add a `hoveredBar` state (string | null). (2) On each bar div, add onMouseEnter={() => setHoveredBar(bar.label)} and onMouseLeave={() => setHoveredBar(null)}. (3) When hoveredBar matches the bar label, show a tooltip div positioned absolutely above the bar containing: the label, the exact formatted value (e.g. '$2,450,000'), and for non-total bars, the percentage of original contract (e.g. '5.2% of original'). Style the tooltip with background white, boxShadow from theme shadows, borderRadius from theme, padding spacing['2'], fontSize typography.fontSize.sm, zIndex 10. (4) Add cursor: 'pointer' to the Approved COs and Pending COs bars. (5) Add aria-label to each bar div: `${bar.label}: ${fmt(bar.value)}`. (6) Add role='img' and aria-label='Contract value waterfall chart showing original contract, approved changes, pending changes, and revised contract total' to the outer container div. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's financial module lacks AIA G702/G703 PDF generation and requires manual Excel workflows for pay apps. SiteSync's computeCurrentPaymentDue function and EarnedValueDashboard with CPI/SPI metrics already exceed Procore's financial depth. However, the retainage calculation bug (issue 004) would produce incorrect payment amounts that a CFO would immediately catch, destroying trust. Fieldwire has zero financial tools. Buildertrend has basic billing but no earned value analysis. SiteSync's treemap budget visualization and waterfall chart are differentiators no competitor offers, but they need mobile responsiveness and accessibility fixes to be field-usable.",
  "summary": "The financial engine module scores 51/100 with strong domain depth in earned value metrics and AIA pay application formulas, but critical gaps in accessibility (SVG charts have no screen reader support), mobile responsiveness (treemap unusable on phones), and a significant calculation bug where retainage is applied to work only instead of the full AIA G702 standard of work plus stored materials. The pay application flow lacks the prerequisite SOV check required by acceptance criteria. The waterfall chart has no interactivity. Fixing the retainage formula is the highest-priority item because incorrect payment calculations will immediately disqualify the product with any CFO. The accessibility and mobile fixes are required for field-first usability. All five issues are surgically scoped to single files with clear before/after states."
}
```
